description: Color: the category the individual hues belong to. Phi had every color and no word for color itself until this one. It also unlocks composition: 'haoni welisha' (voice-color) is timbre, registered in the compound registry.
sound_symbolism: Liquid 'we'-'li' flows into 'sha', the softest of the fricatives: the word washes, as color does.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'mia welisha nila.' (I see color.) 'waero welisha helui.' (The sky's color changes.)

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: The movement thought in color before it thought in line: Tiffany glass, enamel, the mural's palette. This word names their shared medium.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Every color was once a recipe — madder red, woad blue, ochre from the hillside. Naming the category honors the craft that made its members.

tags.aesthetic: What the hues have in common
